ambiguities.description: Participant model (buyers, sellers, vendors, agents, admins) and how they onboard
Product/catalog model (who lists, variants, categories, moderation, digital vs physical)
Discovery and matching (search, browse, recommendations, curated vs algorithmic)
Transaction model (who takes payment, when, what currency, invoicing, netting)
Trust and verification (identity checks, ratings, reviews, dispute resolution, guarantees)
Commission and fee structure (who pays, when, flat vs percentage, tiers)
Fulfillment responsibility (vendor-shipped, platform-shipped, digital delivery, pickup)
Integration with payment, tax, shipping, or accounting systems
Policies (returns, refunds, cancellations, prohibited items, jurisdiction)
Messaging and negotiation (buyer-seller chat, quotes, offers, auctions)
Analytics and reporting (vendor dashboards, platform health, payout reports)
MVP marketplace boundary — what transaction types and features are in scope

questions.question: What participant types must be supported (buyers, sellers, vendors, agents)?
Who is responsible for fulfillment (vendor, platform, hybrid)?
What is the commission or fee model?
What trust and verification mechanisms are required?
What transaction and payment flow is required?
What policies apply (returns, refunds, prohibited items)?
Is buyer-seller messaging or negotiation required?
What external integrations are required (payments, tax, shipping)?

assumptions.assumption: A buyer-and-seller model with platform facilitation is the core MVP pattern
Vendor-managed listings with platform moderation are acceptable initially
Simple percentage-based commission is acceptable for MVP

suggested_closures.decision: Marketplace construction will start with vendor-managed listings, platform-facilitated discovery, and a simple commission model under a conservative trust policy.
suggested_closures.rationale: A focused transaction model with explicit participant responsibilities reduces risk while preserving principal control over fees and fulfillment policies.

seed_tasks.title: Define participant model and onboarding
seed_tasks.transformation: Document buyer, seller, and admin roles, onboarding requirements, and verification levels.
seed_tasks.evidence_requirement: Participant model and onboarding flow exist.
seed_tasks.review_predicate: Every participant type has defined permissions and an onboarding path.

seed_tasks.title: Define product catalog and listing policy
seed_tasks.transformation: Document who can list, what can be listed, category structure, and moderation rules.
seed_tasks.evidence_requirement: Catalog policy and category taxonomy exist.
seed_tasks.review_predicate: Every listing type has a moderation rule; no category is ungoverned.

seed_tasks.title: Define commission and fee structure
seed_tasks.transformation: Document who pays fees, when they are collected, and how payouts work.
seed_tasks.evidence_requirement: Fee structure document exists with examples.
seed_tasks.review_predicate: Every transaction type has a documented fee; no fee is hidden from participants.

seed_tasks.title: Define fulfillment responsibility model
seed_tasks.transformation: Document who ships, who tracks, and who handles returns for each product type.
seed_tasks.review_predicate: Every product type has a clear fulfillment owner.

seed_tasks.title: Define trust, dispute, and refund policies
seed_tasks.transformation: Document verification requirements, dispute resolution steps, and refund conditions.
seed_tasks.evidence_requirement: Trust and dispute policy document exists.
seed_tasks.review_predicate: Every dispute type has a resolution path; every refund condition is objective.

seed_tasks.title: Define MVP marketplace boundary
seed_tasks.transformation: Explicitly list features and transaction types in MVP and what is out of scope.
seed_tasks.evidence_requirement: MVP scope document exists with explicit inclusions and exclusions.
seed_tasks.review_predicate: Scope is achievable and every excluded feature is consciously deferred.

residuals.description: Participant model and onboarding are not defined.
Product catalog and listing policy are not established.
Commission and fee structure are not decided.
Trust and dispute policies are not documented.
Transaction and payment flow is not defined.
